diagnosis: This production run has two distinct root causes related to component installation errors. The findings highlight critical deviations in the assembly process that could lead to functional failures.

members.reason: The installation of an acorn nut instead of the required bracket screw is a critical error that compromises assembly integrity.
Installing the rear wheels before the rear pulley is a significant deviation that can lead to functional issues in the rear axle.
The failure to match the state strip indicates the absence of critical components due to the earlier installation errors.
The omission of the washer in the front wheel assembly is a critical defect that could degrade quality and performance.
